claim: ORIUS provides a reliability-aware runtime safety layer for physical AI under degraded observation, enforcing certificate-backed action release through uncertainty coverage, repair, and fallback.
allowed_language: Reliability-aware runtime safety layer under degraded observation.

claim: Observation-only mandatory-release controllers face a lower bound under safety-relevant ambiguity; ORIUS achieves an alpha-bounded upper guarantee under covered uncertainty sets.
allowed_language: Lower bound for observation-only control; alpha-bounded ORIUS upper guarantee.

claim: The promoted runtime-denominator rows show zero ORIUS TSVR for Battery and Healthcare and epsilon-closed ORIUS TSVR for Autonomous Vehicles under bounded predeployment validation.
boundary: Bounded predeployment validation, not field deployment.
allowed_language: Zero Battery/Healthcare TSVR and epsilon-closed AV TSVR on promoted runtime-denominator rows.

claim: All three promoted domains pass equal artifact-discipline gates.
boundary: Equal artifact discipline is not equal real-world maturity.
allowed_language: Same artifact classes across domains; evidence depth remains tiered.

claim: Battery Energy Storage runtime row reduces TSVR from 0.008333 to 0.000000 with witness pass rate 1.000000.
boundary: Battery remains the witness row. The runtime-governing three-domain lane uses the locked battery witness runtime, while deep battery learned rows remain diagnostic only.
allowed_language: Runtime-denominator safety evidence under the promoted bounded contract.

claim: Autonomous Vehicles runtime row reduces TSVR from 0.289250 to 0.000163 with witness pass rate 0.999837.
boundary: AV is runtime-governed through all-zip grouped nuPlan replay/surrogate runtime-contract evidence. The evidence reports the measured empirical runtime denominator and does not claim road deployment.
allowed_language: Runtime-denominator safety evidence under the promoted bounded contract.

claim: Medical and Healthcare Monitoring runtime row reduces TSVR from 0.194489 to 0.000000 with witness pass rate 1.000000.
boundary: Healthcare is runtime-governed on the full promoted MIMIC denominator under the fail-safe release contract. The validation harness remains a secondary proxy surface only.
allowed_language: Runtime-denominator safety evidence under the promoted bounded contract.

claim: T4 is part of the defended necessity and covered-safety theorem chain.
boundary: Defended as the observation-necessity witness for the fixed-margin quality-ignorant controller class on the battery row with explicit arbitrage reachability.
allowed_language: Defended theorem chain with explicit scope limits.

claim: T10_T11_ObservationAmbiguitySandwich is part of the defended necessity and covered-safety theorem chain.
boundary: Supporting optimality corollary under covered observation ambiguity; contract-universal, not unrestricted-global; it does not assert unrestricted global optimality for all physical AI systems.
allowed_language: Defended theorem chain with explicit scope limits.

claim: T11 is part of the defended necessity and covered-safety theorem chain.
boundary: Defended as the forward four-obligation one-step transfer theorem; the converse remains a separate structural failure proposition.
allowed_language: Defended theorem chain with explicit scope limits.

claim: Battery Energy Storage has software_hil_rehearsal evidence on battery_hil_or_simulator.
boundary: HIL/simulator rehearsal, not unrestricted field deployment.
allowed_language: Predeployment validation only; do not call it unrestricted deployment.

claim: Autonomous Vehicles has bounded_kinematic_closed_loop_planner evidence on nuplan_bounded_kinematic_closed_loop_planner.
boundary: Bounded kinematic nuPlan closed-loop planner evaluation: ego actions update future ego speed and gap, but this is not CARLA, road deployment, or full autonomous-driving field closure.
allowed_language: Predeployment validation only; do not call it unrestricted deployment.

claim: Medical and Healthcare Monitoring has retrospective_time_forward_and_source_holdout_ready evidence on healthcare_retrospective_time_forward_source_holdout.
boundary: Retrospective source-holdout/time-forward split validation, not prospective trial evidence and not live clinical deployment.
allowed_language: Predeployment validation only; do not call it unrestricted deployment.

claim: German OPSD training surface is verified with primary target load_mw, RMSE 256.30623919216544, and PICP90 0.9301587301587302.
boundary: Model quality supports runtime evidence but is not the safety claim by itself.
allowed_language: Training quality is reported separately from runtime release safety.

claim: nuPlan All-Zip Grouped AV Replay training surface is verified with primary target ego_speed_mps_1s, RMSE , and PICP90 0.9345008568980292.
boundary: Model quality supports runtime evidence but is not the safety claim by itself.
allowed_language: Training quality is reported separately from runtime release safety.

claim: Healthcare Vital Signs training surface is verified with primary target hr_bpm, RMSE 1.353953089494559, and PICP90 0.90297659622813.
boundary: Model quality supports runtime evidence but is not the safety claim by itself.
allowed_language: Training quality is reported separately from runtime release safety.

claim: The active max offline freeze is not claimed complete until a predeployment release manifest exists.
boundary: No final freeze claim before predeployment_release_manifest.json exists.
allowed_language: Freeze is complete only when the release manifest and hash locks exist.
